nouns.summary: YHWH is described as taking delight in the subject being crushed and made ill. The verse also states that YHWH’s pleasure is connected to what happens through the person’s hand, and that this leads to success/prospering.
“Delight/pleasure” is presented first as something YHWH has regarding the subject’s crushing. It appears again as YHWH’s pleasure being active in the person’s hand, tied to the resulting prospering.
The verse says that if the person sets his guilt (as an offering), then this condition leads to what follows in the person’s experience—specifically seeing offspring/seed and living longer.
The person’s “soul” is portrayed as being placed as part of the guilt-offering situation. This action is linked to the subsequent outcomes: the person will see seed/offspring and will prolong days.
The verse connects “seed” to the person’s future: after the guilt-offering condition, the person will see seed/offspring.
The text states that the person will prolong “days,” meaning their lifespan/length of life increases as part of the outcome tied to the preceding condition.
YHWH’s pleasure is said to be “in his hand,” and from that placement the outcome is that what is in the hand prospers/succeeds.
